packages/identity/uuid7/overview.html.title: Dixor.Identity | Dixor Documentation
packages/identity/uuid7/overview.html.summary: Dixor.Identity 📚 Navigation: Home > Dixor.Identity > Dixor.Identity Overview Dixor.Identity delivers modern, allocation-conscious identifier generation for .NET. While standard .NET applications rely on Guid.NewGuid() (UUID Version 4), random keys cause database fragmentation, index page splits, and cache misses in high-throughput workloads. Dixor.Identity solves this by implementing RFC 9562 UUID Version 7, embedding a 48-bit millisecond Unix timestamp with cryptographically secure randomness for natural chronological sorting and global uniqueness. Why Choose Dixor.Identity? Feature Legacy Guid.NewGuid() (UUIDv4) Dixor.Identity (Uuid7.NewGuid()) Clustered Index Performance ❌ Causes Page Splits & Fragmentation ✅ Sequential B-Tree Leaf Append Chronologically Sortable ❌ No ✅ Yes (Natural Sort Order) Embedded Creation Time ❌ No ✅ Yes (Extractable with zero DB lookups) Memory Allocation Medium (Heap/Buffer overhead) ✅ Zero-Allocation Hot Paths (stackalloc) Same-Millisecond Ordering ❌ Random ✅ Guaranteed via Monotonic Sequencing RFC Compliance RFC 4122 (Legacy) ✅ RFC 9562 (Modern Enterprise Standard) Core Capabilities at a Glance using Dixor.Identity.UUID7; // 1. Standard Generation: Mint a zero-allocation, time-ordered primary key Guid primaryKey = Uuid7.NewGuid(); // 2. Monotonic Sequencing: Guarantee strict mathematical order across same-millisecond bursts Guid eventId = Uuid7.NewMonotonicGuid(); // 3. Batch Streaming: Lazily yield 5,000 keys for ETL pipelines without heap bloat IEnumerable<Guid> batch = Uuid7.NewGuids(count: 5000); // 4. Timestamp Extraction: Decode inception chronology directly from the ID payload DateTimeOffset createdAt = Uuid7.GetTimestamp(primaryKey); // 5. Domain Formatting: Decorate keys with business prefixes and safe separators string invoiceRef = Uuid7.NewString(prefix: "INV", suffix: "EU"); // 6. Safe Parsing & Validation: Verify untrusted HTTP headers or log strings if (Uuid7.IsValid("ORD-0197d8ef-f4c6-7a35-99e3-1d4a7e3c8e10")) { Guid orderId = Uuid7.Parse("ORD-0197d8ef-f4c6-7a35-99e3-1d4a7e3c8e10"); } Note 💡 Interactive Demonstration: Try out generation parameters and inspect performance metrics directly via Dixor Studio. Deep-Dive Documentation Guides UUID Version 7 Overview — RFC 9562 anatomy and bitwise specifications. Production Best Practices — Optimal database schema design and EF Core integration. Standard Generation — Zero-allocation stack mechanics (Uuid7.NewGuid). Monotonic Generation — Sub-millisecond ordering (Uuid7.NewMonotonicGuid). Batch Generation — Lazy memory streaming (Uuid7.NewGuids). Business Formatting — Custom prefixes and separators (Uuid7.NewString). Validation Rules — Fast-path nibble inspection (Uuid7.IsValid). Parsing & Extraction — Regex pattern matching (Uuid7.Parse). Timestamp Extraction — Decoding time chronology (Uuid7.GetTimestamp). References & Credits Specification: Built strictly around the IETF RFC 9562 Standard for Universally Unique Identifiers (UUIDs). Inspiration: Special acknowledgment to foundational .NET UUIDv7 ecosystem standards and community specifications for high-performance allocation strategies.

packages/validation/ValidationResult.htm.title: ValidationResult Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/ValidationResult.htm.summary: ValidationResult Reference The ValidationResult<TError> is a generic, high-performance record struct used throughout the Dixor.Validation library to return the outcome of any validation operation. Overview By using a record struct, this type provides value-type semantics (avoiding heap allocations) while maintaining immutability and easy equality checks. It forces callers to explicitly handle success or failure states via the IsValid property. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-05-07 Change Logs: Created immutable ValidationResult record struct for allocation-free outcomes. Implemented generic error support using constraint-based Enum types. Added factory methods for Success and Failure states. Usage The result is designed for simple, idiomatic usage within service methods: public ValidationResult<EmailValidationError> Validate(string email) { if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(email)) return ValidationResult<EmailValidationError>.Failure(EmailValidationError.Empty); return ValidationResult<EmailValidationError>.Success(); } // Consumer consumption var result = validator.Validate("test@example.com"); if (result.IsValid) { // Proceed... } else { Console.WriteLine($"Error: {result.Error}"); }

packages/validation/email/DomainValidato.title: DomainValidator Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/DomainValidato.summary: DomainValidator Reference The DomainValidator is an internal service within the Dixor.Validation.Email library responsible for the syntactical verification of the domain portion of an email address. Overview This validator ensures that the domain part of an email adheres to structural and length requirements defined by internet standards. It functions independently of the local part validation. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-05-07 Change Logs: Created domain syntax validation logic. Added length validation for domain and domain labels. Added structural validation (dots, hyphens). Added character validation with internationalization support. Validation Logic The validator executes three primary checks: Length Validation: Ensures the total domain string length does not exceed EmailDomainMaxLength. Structural Validation (ValidateLabels): Prevents .. sequences. Prevents domain names starting or ending with a dot. Ensures every individual label (part separated by dots) is non-empty and follows length constraints. Validates label boundary characters (prevents starting or ending with a hyphen). Character Validation: Permits ASCII letters, digits, dots, and hyphens by default. If AllowInternational is set to true in EmailValidationOptions, it permits Unicode characters (non-ASCII).

packages/validation/email/EmailInfo.html.title: EmailInfo Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailInfo.html.summary: EmailInfo Reference The EmailInfo class provides a structured, immutable representation of a parsed email address within the Dixor.Validation.Email library. Overview EmailInfo stores the granular components of an email address. By parsing these elements once, the library enables high-performance validation and inspection without redundant string manipulation. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-04-07 Change Logs: Created immutable representation of a parsed email address. Added properties for local part, domain and domain components. Added support for plus-address detection. Added support for quoted local-part detection. Added support for IP-literal domain detection. Component Breakdown Property Description Example (from user+tag@mail.sub.example.com) Value The full input string. user+tag@mail.sub.example.com LocalPart The user-defined part before @. user+tag Domain The full host part after @. mail.sub.example.com SecondLevelDomain The main domain segment. example TopLevelDomain The suffix (TLD). com Subdomain The host/sub-server part. mail.sub Feature Flags HasPlusAddressing: Detects sub-addressing (the + character used for filtering). IsQuotedLocalPart: Detects if the local part is wrapped in quotes (standard-compliant method to allow special characters). IsIpDomain: Detects if the domain is formatted as an IP address literal (e.g., [127.0.0.1]) rather than a domain name.

packages/validation/email/EmailNormalize.title: EmailNormalizer Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailNormalize.summary: EmailNormalizer Reference The EmailNormalizer is an internal service within the Dixor.Validation.Email library designed to transform email addresses into a canonical, consistent format. Overview Normalization ensures that two email addresses that are semantically identical (e.g., User@Example.com and User@example.com) are represented by the same string. This service focuses on safe, non-destructive transformations. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-05-07 Change Logs: Created email normalization service for canonical form conversion. Implemented whitespace trimming and Unicode normalization (Form C). Added domain-specific case-insensitivity (lowercasing). Normalization Steps The Normalize method performs the following operations in order: Trimming: Removes any leading or trailing whitespace from the input string. Unicode Canonicalization: Applies NormalizationForm.FormC to ensure characters are represented in a standard, composed form. Domain Case Folding: Identifies the domain portion (after the last @ symbol). Converts the domain to lowercase (ToLowerInvariant). Note: The local part is preserved as-is, as some email providers treat the local part as case-sensitive. Reconstruction: Concatenates the original local part and the newly normalized domain.

packages/validation/email/EmailParser.ht.title: EmailParser Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailParser.ht.summary: EmailParser Reference The EmailParser is the central engine of the Dixor.Validation.Email library, coordinating the end-to-end processing pipeline for email validation and decomposition. Overview The parser transforms a raw string into a structured EmailInfo object by executing a series of validation stages. If any stage fails, it returns a specific EmailValidationError through the ValidationResult. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-04-07 Change Logs: Created the initial email parsing pipeline. Added input validation. Added email normalization. Added local-part and domain validation. Added extraction of email components. Added generation of EmailInfo metadata. The Parsing Pipeline Parsing occurs in the following sequential order: Input Validation: Ensures the string is not null, empty, or just whitespace. Normalization: Canonicalizes the email using the EmailNormalizer (trimming, Unicode normalization, case folding). Decomposition: Splits the string at the @ symbol, ensuring exactly one separator exists. Component Validation: Invokes ILocalPartValidator to verify the local part syntax. Invokes IDomainValidator to verify domain syntax. Metadata Extraction: Constructs the EmailInfo object, including: Domain hierarchy (TLD, SLD, Subdomain). Feature detection (Plus addressing, Quoted local parts, IP literals).

packages/validation/email/EmailValidatio.title: EmailValidationError Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailValidatio.summary: EmailValidationError Reference The EmailValidationError enumeration defines a comprehensive set of reasons for email validation failures within the Dixor.Validation.Email library. Overview This enum is used primarily by ValidationResult<EmailValidationError> to provide granular, deterministic feedback when an email address fails validation checks. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-02-07 Change Logs: Created the initial email validation error enumeration. Added validation errors for null, empty, and whitespace inputs. Added syntax validation errors for local parts and domains. Added validation errors for quoted local parts and plus addressing. Added validation errors for international characters and IP domains. Error Categories Input & Basic Structure None: Success. Null, Empty, WhiteSpace: Issues with the raw input string. TooLong: Total address exceeds length limits. MissingAtSymbol, MultipleAtSymbols: Parsing failures for the address separator. Local Part Issues EmptyLocalPart, LocalPartTooLong: Size constraints. LocalPartStartsWithDot, LocalPartEndsWithDot, ConsecutiveDots: Formatting rules for local parts. InvalidLocalPartCharacter: Illegal symbols detected. Domain & TLD Issues EmptyDomain, DomainTooLong, DomainLabelTooLong: Size constraints. DomainStartsWithDot, DomainEndsWithDot: Formatting rules. DomainLabelStartsWithHyphen, DomainLabelEndsWithHyphen: Formatting rules for domain segments. InvalidDomain, InvalidTopLevelDomain, InvalidDomainCharacter: Syntax and TLD validity checks. Policy & Advanced Features QuotedLocalPartNotAllowed, InvalidQuotedLocalPart: Handling of quoted syntax. PlusAddressingNotAllowed: Handling of sub-addressing. InvalidIpDomain: IP literal validation. InternationalCharactersNotAllowed: Unicode/EAI configuration checks. NotAllowed: General policy rejection.

packages/validation/email/EmailValidatio.title: EmailValidationOptions Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailValidatio.summary: EmailValidationOptions Reference The EmailValidationOptions class provides a configuration object that allows developers to customize the validation logic of the EmailValidator class. Overview By modifying the properties of an EmailValidationOptions instance, you can tailor the validation process to meet specific business requirements, such as restricting disposable emails or enabling specific formats like plus addressing. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-02-07 Change Logs: Created the initial set of email validation and inspection APIs. Added email normalization endpoints. Added email component retrieval endpoints. Added domain matching endpoints. Added normalized email comparison support. Configuration Properties Property Type Default Description AllowInternational bool true Permits international (Unicode/EAI) email addresses. AllowPlusAddressing bool true Permits plus addressing (e.g., user+tag@domain.com). AllowQuotedLocalPart bool false Permits quoted strings in the local part (e.g., "john smith"@domain.com). AllowLocalhost bool false Permits localhost as a domain. AllowIpDomain bool false Permits IP address domain literals (e.g., user@[127.0.0.1]). AllowDisposableDomains bool true Permits domains associated with temporary/disposable email services. AllowPublicDomains bool true Permits public email providers (e.g., Gmail, Outlook). AllowRoleBasedAddresses bool true Permits role-based accounts (e.g., admin@, support@). TrimInput bool true Automatically trims leading/trailing whitespace before validation. IgnoreDomainCase bool true Treats domain names as case-insensitive. Usage Example var options = new EmailValidationOptions { AllowDisposableDomains = false, AllowPlusAddressing = false }; bool isValid = EmailValidator.IsValid("test@mailinator.com", options); // Returns false

packages/validation/email/EmailValidator.title: EmailValidator Documentation | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailValidator.summary: EmailValidator Documentation Overview EmailValidator is the primary entry point for working with email addresses in the Dixor.Validation library. It provides a static, thread-safe API to validate, normalize, and inspect email address components. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-02-07 Change Logs: Created the initial set of email validation and inspection APIs. Added email normalization endpoints. Added email component retrieval endpoints. Added domain matching endpoints. Added normalized email comparison support. API Reference 1. Email Validation Endpoints Function 1: IsValid Determines whether the specified email address is valid. Function 2: Validate Validates the specified email address and returns a detailed ValidationResult. 2. Email Normalizing Endpoints Function 3: Normalize Normalizes the specified email address to its canonical form. Function 4: TryNormalize Attempts to normalize the specified email address, returning the result via an out parameter. 3. Email Information Endpoints Function 5: GetInfo Retrieves detailed information (as an EmailInfo instance) about the specified email address. 4. Email Component Endpoints Function 6: GetLocalPart Retrieves the local part of the specified email address. Function 7: TryGetLocalPart Attempts to retrieve the local part of the specified email address. Function 8: GetDomain Retrieves the domain portion of the specified email address. Function 9: TryGetDomain Attempts to retrieve the domain portion of the specified email address. Function 10: GetSubDomain Retrieves the subdomain portion if one exists. Function 11: TryGetSubDomain Attempts to retrieve the subdomain portion if one exists. 5. Email Domain Matching Endpoints Function 12: HasDomain Checks if the email belongs to a specific domain. Function 13: HasAnyDomain Checks if the email belongs to any domain in a provided collection. Function 14: HasTopLevelDomain Checks if the email uses a specific top-level domain (TLD). 6. Email Comparison Endpoints Function 15: AreEqual Determines if two email addresses are equivalent after normalization.

packages/validation/email/EmailValidator.title: EmailValidatorService Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/EmailValidator.summary: EmailValidatorService Reference The EmailValidatorService is the internal orchestration layer for the Dixor.Validation.Email library. It acts as the primary bridge between the public EmailValidator API and the specialized internal services. Overview This service does not contain validation logic itself. Instead, it coordinates calls to IEmailParser and IEmailNormalizer. This ensures a single, consistent execution pipeline for all validation, normalization, and inspection tasks. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-04-07 Change Logs: Created the initial email validation service implementation. Added email validation endpoints. Added email normalization endpoints. Added email information retrieval endpoints. Added email component retrieval endpoints. Added email domain matching endpoints. Added normalized email comparison support. Integrated the service with the library's internal dependency injection container. Responsibilities Validation Coordination: Executes the parsing pipeline via IEmailParser. Normalization Gateway: Exposes IEmailNormalizer functionality for consistent string representation. Component Access: Simplifies the retrieval of specific email parts (LocalPart, Domain, Subdomain) by wrapping EmailInfo extraction. Domain Logic: Implements domain matching and TLD checks using case-insensitive comparisons. Comparison Logic: Facilitates equality checks by normalizing both inputs before comparison.

packages/validation/email/LocalPartValid.title: LocalPartValidator Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/LocalPartValid.summary: LocalPartValidator Reference The LocalPartValidator is an internal service within the Dixor.Validation.Email library responsible for syntactical and policy-based verification of the "local part" of an email address (the segment before the @ symbol). Overview This validator ensures the local part adheres to formatting rules defined by RFC 5321/5322 and respects the custom EmailValidationOptions provided by the consumer. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-05-07 Change Logs: Created local-part syntax validation logic. Added length constraints and dot-sequence validation. Implemented policy checks for quoted strings and plus addressing. Added character set validation with international support. Validation Rules Length: Must not exceed EmailLocalPartMaxLength. Dot Positioning: No consecutive dots (..). Cannot start or end with a dot. Policy Controls: Quoted Strings: Checks if the local part is enclosed in ""; rejected if AllowQuotedLocalPart is false. Plus Addressing: Checks for + character; rejected if AllowPlusAddressing is false. Character Set: Strictly validates against a whitelist of ASCII special characters allowed in email local parts. If AllowInternational is enabled, it permits Unicode characters (non-ASCII) beyond the standard set.

packages/validation/email/ValidationCons.title: ValidationConstants Reference | Dixor Documentation
packages/validation/email/ValidationCons.summary: ValidationConstants Reference The ValidationConstants class is an internal static utility that defines the architectural constraints and character literals used by the Dixor.Validation.Email library. Overview By centralizing these values, the library ensures that all internal validators (such as EmailParser, LocalPartValidator, and DomainValidator) use consistent boundaries, strictly adhering to RFC 5321 and RFC 5322 specifications. Authorship and Versioning Author: Dishant Prashant Save Original Created Date: 2026-05-07 Change Logs: Created centralized constants for email validation rules. Added RFC-compliant length limits for email components. Added static character constants for parsing operations. Configuration Constants Length Constraints These limits define the structural boundaries of a valid email address as implemented by the library's parsing logic: Constant Value Description EmailMaxLength 254 Total length of the full email address. EmailLocalPartMaxLength 64 Maximum length of the local-part. EmailDomainMaxLength 253 Maximum length of the domain portion. DomainLabelMaxLength 63 Maximum length of a single segment (label) in a domain. Special Characters Used by internal validators to avoid "magic strings" and ensure consistent character identification during tokenization and parsing: Separators: At ('@'), `Dot` ('.'). Feature Flags: Plus ('+'), Quote ('"'). Domain Literals: OpenBracket ('['), CloseBracket (']').
